title: acaena elongata var. lappacea
intro: Acaena elongata var. lappacea, also known as New Zealand bugweed or silver pine, is a perennial plant native to New Zealand and parts of Australia. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful for wound healing and reducing swelling. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing fever, relieving coughs, and treating digestive issues. Acaena elongata var. lappacea can be prepared as a herbal tea or infusion, as well as being used in traditional remedies such as poultices and salves.
